replication_study.hypothesis: In a single-letter recognition task with backward masking, trials preceded by an auditory alerting cue (as implemented in these logs) will show higher TVA processing speed v and a lower perceptual threshold t0 compared to trials without a cue (within-subject comparison).

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory behavioral experiment (E-Prime 2 exports); single-letter recognition with backward masking
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Replication of Experiment 1 design: No cue vs Cue; two sessions per subject (Day1 and Day2).
data_plan.sample_size: Approximately 35–45 participants × 2 sessions each; exact N computed at runtime from available subject-day files.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant (subject-level TVA parameters estimated from trial-level accuracy).
data_plan.access_details: Excel logs are provided under replication_data; before running, copy or mount them to /app/data inside the container (e.g., host ./data -> /app/data).

qualification.explanation: This dataset is an independent replication sample collected with the same task structure (single-letter recognition, backward masking, auditory cue vs no cue). It enables a high-fidelity test of the focal claim on new participants while preserving the original design.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original paper (original_paper.pdf) Experiment 1 design specifies: single-letter recognition, 8 exposure durations (10–80 ms), No cue vs 85 dB cue with constant-hazard foreperiods; TVA parameters v and t0 estimated via Nelder–Mead (Sections 3.1.2, 3.1.4; Results 3.2). post_registration.json reiterates these details (method.description, models, outcome_variable). The replication Excel logs follow the same E-Prime output convention parsed by the provided MATLAB scripts (analysis_Petersen2017_v5–v7.m) and include per-trial lines with 'target:' and 'Sound' (replication_data/*.xlsx; parsing approach detailed in analysis_Petersen2017_v7.m). Thus, the replication data share the same operationalization of trials, exposure durations, and cue presence needed to estimate TVA parameters.
qualification.deviation_from_original: This is a new sample collected in 2020–2021 (see filenames, e.g., 1901_Day1_10-24-2020-VAT_replication_scenario.xlsx), whereas the original experiments were conducted earlier in Copenhagen (original_paper.pdf). The replication logs may not encode cue intensity explicitly per trial; they implement No cue vs Cue, allowing replication of Experiment 1 but not the intensity gradation of Experiments 2–3. Individual_Data_Descriptions.txt documents subject-level anomalies (e.g., negative t0 estimates, headphone issues, unhealthy hearing exclusions), indicating differences in sample composition and data quality profile relative to the original (post_registration.json notes screened normal hearing; original_paper.pdf Section 3.1.1). We will exclude or handle problematic subjects via listwise deletion for paired tests as per our analysis plan.

data_plan.notes: File naming includes minor variations and spaces (e.g., '1912_Day1_12-14-2020 -VAT_replication_scenario.xlsx'); the Python code uses glob patterns '[####]_Day[12]_*.xlsx' and is robust to such differences. Negative t0 estimates can occur (noted in Individual_Data_Descriptions.txt) due to model fitting; we retain estimates as the original model permits t0 <= 0 for some fits. Pupillometry is not present; this replication focuses on behavioral TVA parameters only. Ensure all Excel logs are available under /app/data before execution.

steps: Place all Excel logs named '####_Day1_*.xlsx' and '####_Day2_*.xlsx' into /app/data.

The script will: (a) discover subject files; (b) parse trial records (identify trials by 'target:' and cue presence by 'Sound'); (c) aggregate accuracy by exposure duration for Cue vs NoCue; (d) fit TVA parameters (v, t0, pg) per subject-condition using Nelder–Mead; (e) compute paired t-tests for NoCue vs Cue on v and on t0; (f) save outputs to /app/data (replication_tva_params.csv and replication_ttests.json).
Inspect replication_ttests.json and compare direction and significance to the original report.
planned_method.models: TVA-based single-letter encoding model with parameters v (processing speed), t0 (perceptual threshold), and pg (guess rate). Probability model: p = (1 - exp(-v*(s - t0))) + exp(-v*(s - t0))*pg*(1/20). Parameters estimated via Nelder–Mead (sum of squared residuals) separately for NoCue and Cue.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Primary outcomes: processing speed v (letters/s) and perceptual threshold t0 (ms), estimated per subject for NoCue and Cue.
planned_method.independent_variables: Presence of auditory alerting cue (Cue vs NoCue).
planned_method.control_variables: Within-subject design; exposure durations (10–80 ms) are aggregated per condition; Day1/Day2 combined within subject. Cue frequency/intensity are not analyzed (not encoded), aligning with Experiment 1 replication.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Subject-level TVA parameters (v, t0, pg) per condition; paired differences in v and t0 (NoCue vs Cue).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided paired-sample t-tests comparing NoCue vs Cue for v and for t0 across subjects.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Per-parameter listwise deletion: for each test (v or t0), include only subjects with valid estimates in both conditions; durations with missing data are dropped pairwise within each subject-condition fit.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable; two focal outcomes pre-specified by the original study; we report uncorrected p-values as in the original.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Alpha = 0.05 (two-sided). Expected direction: v_Cue > v_NoCue (positive effect on speed), t0_Cue < t0_NoCue (lower threshold).

files.replication_data/analysis_Petersen2017_v: Python translation of the MATLAB analysis for Experiment 1. Discovers Excel logs in /app/data, parses trial-level records, aggregates accuracy by exposure duration for Cue vs NoCue, fits TVA parameters (v, t0, pg) via Nelder–Mead (scipy.optimize.minimize), computes paired t-tests (scipy.stats.ttest_rel) for NoCue vs Cue on v and on t0, and writes outputs to /app/data (replication_tva_params.csv and replication_ttests.json).

codebase.notes: This is a faithful Python rewrite of analysis_Petersen2017_v7.m with IO redirected to /app/data per run policy. Original MATLAB scripts remain unchanged for reference. Dependencies: pandas (Excel IO and data handling), openpyxl (Excel engine), numpy (arrays), scipy (optimization and t-tests). No random seeds are used; Nelder–Mead may have minor numerical variation but should replicate the qualitative pattern.

instructions: Ensure all Excel logs (e.g., 1901_Day1_10-24-2020-VAT_replication_scenario.xlsx and corresponding Day2 files for each subject) are available on the host under ./data so they mount to /app/data.
Build/run container with volume: docker run -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data -it <image> bash
Outputs: /app/data/replication_tva_params.csv (subject-level v, t0, pg), /app/data/replication_ttests.json (summary with paired t for v and t0).
comparison_metrics: Direction of effects: v_Cue > v_NoCue; t0_Cue < t0_NoCue.
Statistical significance: paired t-tests compared to original (Exp. 1 reported t(27)=4.71, p<0.001 for v; t(27)=3.52, p=0.002 for t0; original_paper.pdf Section 3.2; post_registration.json results).
Magnitude comparison: mean differences in v and t0 between conditions; report t-statistics and p-values.
Robustness: proportion of subjects contributing valid paired estimates; note any exclusions due to missing or anomalous fits.
